reviews.review_title: I hope you have more space than me
reviews.review_description: So much fun, I will be buying the game. You definitely need space to play to get the most out of it. Like 10'x10' at least. I kept banging my hands into a wall corner & for some reason, it wanted me to stay in the worst possible, tiniest area.. Even tho I set my room up properly. I still don't know if you play against other people (I hope) or just bots.
Game is super awesome, has options for area scale enlargement for those in small play areas.
The controles are very hard if your using hand traking
Love the immersion.
Played through it. played through it again. played through it again… Got good cardio from standing and running from enemies for hours on end
I ended up buying the full game on Steam and not here, but it's the pride of my VR library. The first impossible spaces game in modern VR, and no less remarkable for it.

I sort of like to see this as a “what humanity will become if we keep going in the direction that we’re going” kinda feel. Playing as a humanoid robot gives this game the strange sensation that you are one of the last things that tell the world that humans lived there. 10/10
If you got here by any chance just try it, great idea and execution, really what VR should be going to in terms of gaming! Kudos to the devs!

It would be five stars but on a recent update they removed everything making the demo great. You used to be able to progress all the way in the story until the scene where you use the AA gun to take out the enemy ships. Now you spawn in walk along the doors it forces you to, fight two enemies, then get booted to the menu. I really think that 1 it makes it much less enjoyable and 2 makes the free demo make the game look bad. Also you used to be able to just explore and mess around.
Just five stars
Everytime i boot this game up I get very hard
THIS IS THE MOST TRASH GAME I'VE EVER PLAYED 0 PEOPLE HAVE MADE A VR SHOOTER THAT DOESN'T HAVE A  AUTO WALK SO PUT THAT IN THE GAME SO IT'S ACTUALLY PLAYABLE IDIOTS

reviews.review_title: Clever but lacks the fun factor
reviews.review_description:
This is a very clever idea but it's no fun to play. The atmosphere is very grim and the way the gun shoots is not fun.  Also there is a lot of trial and error involved while playing.  I couldn't make sense of the map either.  To cap it all, it seems that some of the enemies respawn.  

They say it needs a 2m by 2m minimum space but that wasn't enough for me.  The game needs a lot of improvements.

Have just getting my oculus I was fairly disappointed with the lack of a variety of games on the market and was having trouble finding one that was intriguing, but once I found this that was it. This is easy the best game on the store even though it’s unfinished, the graphics are a bit disappointing but gameplay, such as actually walking on your own two feet was amazing, it makes my whole play space feel massive vs restrictive. Also the moment I figured out that hand tracking was a thing it took the whole game to another level. Being able to interact in the game with out your controllers literally weighing you down was amazing, and added another since of realism to a already amazing game. Any way, very exited for the full release and I will definitely recommend this game to others.

They should make more impossible room games like this as it's very immersive actually walking around instead of just teleporting or using thumb sticks. There's only 2 of these types of games on the Quest 2 atm; this and Travrsal, they are both in development too so nothing that's been officially released yet. There's some for PCVR though if you have a powerful enough PC.

Tip, download the lastest builds from Discord for more content.

It would be really cool if in the final game spaceships would dock at one point in the story, and you could walk onto it. From their the interior could have a different color scheme. Also I hope the opening train sequence will be longer

developer_response.body: I have a plan prepared for the whole game and... you should not be disappointed.

reviews.review_description: Really love the way the game makes your play area an endless maze that will have you walking around looking crazy.

This game is an absolute must-see*.

* There are issues with this game, particularly in the opening sequence, but that is no different to saying there are issues with VR software in general. The experience which the proper meat of this game pulls off is comparable to the likes of Super-Hot.

There are bugs in this game, many bugs, many many bugs, none of which I need to go into detail here, but I will say that I am very impressed. This game, as much of a demo as it is, is a masterpiece of its time, but there is still so, so much more that it could do so much better at, yet at the same time, nothing else compares in this day.
I write this review intending it to age well, it is 4 stars today, it will be 4.9 stars this time next year.
In the mean time, Demo-Roguelike mode will give you hours upon hours of playtime and virtual experiences.

i r8 8/8 GR8 VR M8!
Loved the illusion of winding my way though big world. Not much to do yet but looks promising 👍🏻

Comfort rating: poor. I got motion sickness fast and then the strobing lightning or whatever made me rip the headset off. At that point I think the game crashed and my headset started screeching at a high pitch. Could be fun, but not for those who get migraines.

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear that. I myself get migraines as well as I get motion sick in the games that I haven't (and sometimes the other way around). That's why the comfort of the players is really important to me. Hence the comfort options when the game is loading.

As the game has some sections that might induce motion sickness. I want to keep it as mild as possible. But also I want the player to setup the game as soon as possible, right in the beginning (the travel on the aircraft) and allow adjusting it right away (that's why there's the display and the handle to pull).

Your (bad) experience is important as it means the game needs changes. Both with communication and with the setup.
